market_note: Open USD (OUSD) — a new dollar stablecoin backed by 140+ firms including Stripe, Visa, Mastercard, and BlackRock — went live on Solana this week, one of the biggest challenges yet to Tether and Circle's dominance.

stablecoin_items.headline: Stripe, Visa, Mastercard, BlackRock launch rival stablecoin Open USD
stablecoin_items.summary: A coalition of 140+ banks and fintechs launched Open USD (OUSD) on Solana, passing reserve yield back to businesses that mint and hold the token rather than keeping it as issuer profit.

stablecoin_items.headline: EU MiCA transitional period ends, full authorization now required
stablecoin_items.summary: As of July 1, every Crypto-Asset Service Provider in the EU must hold full MiCA authorization or shut down; penalties reach 10% of global turnover or €10M, plus personal liability for senior managers.

stablecoin_items.headline: GENIUS Act rulemaking deadline looms July 18
stablecoin_items.summary: Six U.S. federal agencies tasked with finalizing stablecoin rules under the GENIUS Act have until July 18 to publish the joint regulatory framework.
stablecoin_items.source: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency

stablecoin_items.headline: Tether launches stablecoin wallet; Circle's Arc chain moves to proof-of-stake
stablecoin_items.summary: Tether rolled out its own consumer stablecoin wallet while Circle continued transitioning its Arc chain to a proof-of-stake consensus model.

hack_items.headline: KelpDAO exploit hits $294M, among 2026's largest
hack_items.summary: Investigators describe the KelpDAO breach as a sophisticated, state-directed operation exploiting infrastructure rather than a simple smart-contract bug.

hack_items.headline: Drift Protocol breach drains roughly $285M
hack_items.summary: TRM Labs attributes the Drift Protocol loss to an infrastructure compromise; combined with KelpDAO, the two incidents account for nearly 59% of all H1 2026 crypto theft.

hack_items.headline: North Korea-linked groups tied to 66% of H1 2026 crypto theft
hack_items.summary: Crypto hacks hit record frequency in H1 2026, though total losses stayed just under $1B; DPRK-linked actors are estimated behind roughly $643M of that total.

hack_items.headline: Coordinated attack drains 100+ EVM wallets for $107K
hack_items.summary: A mysterious, automated attack swept across EVM-compatible chains, draining small amounts from over 100 wallets in a pattern researchers are still tracing.
